commit.message:
Sprint 17: FieldId null guard, undo laws 1-6, CI hardening, theme integration

- Remove [<Struct>] from FieldId; add null guard in FieldId.create
- Fix benchmark CI: 500%->150% threshold, needs build-and-test, 15 iterations,
  split into regression (read-only) + push-baseline (write, master-only) jobs
- Fix SingleLineContent.arb: generator-first approach, eliminate Arb.filter
  shrinking defect; expand ContentMsg char set + weighted frequency
- Fix vacuous 'false -> true' branches in Laws 1 and 3
- Add Law 5: content-changing edit after undo clears Future
- Add Law 6: depth-2 multi-step undo roundtrip
- Add theme integration: ProgressBar.withTheme, TextInput.viewThemed,
  TextInput.viewWithPlaceholderThemed, Checkbox.viewThemed, Toggle.viewThemed,
  Tabs.withTheme, VirtualList.withTheme (all additive, zero breaking changes)
- Add 19 themed widget tests (1,551 total, all passing)
- Update README test count 1,532 -> 1,551

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move completion comment into shared evaluation-run workflow (#286)

Move the 'Post completion comment' step from evaluation-fork-pr.yml into
evaluation-run.yml as a dedicated post-completion-comment job, so both
evaluation.yml and evaluation-fork-pr.yml share the same logic.

The fork-pr workflow retains a slim step that only handles the skipped case
(no entries / discover failed) since evaluation-run.yml isn't invoked then.

Also extract the '<!-- skill-validator-results -->' marker into a workflow-
level PR_COMMENT_MARKER env var to eliminate duplication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Repository for skills to assist AI coding agents with .NET and C#

commit.message:
Fix infra change detection to use merge-base instead of base tip (#285)

The evaluation workflow was using a two-point diff (base_sha..head_sha) to
detect changed files, which includes files changed on main that the PR branch
hasn't merged yet. This caused false 'Infrastructure changes detected' triggers
when evaluation workflow files were modified on main after a PR branch diverged.

Switch to computing the merge-base first, so only changes actually introduced
by the PR are considered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CI step to run examples against ClickHouse and fix example errors (#601)

## Summary

Adds a CI job that executes all compatible examples in `./examples`
against a live ClickHouse instance (both `head` and `latest`), and fixes
two runtime bugs discovered in the process.

### CI / runner script

- **`examples/run-examples.mjs`** — new script that runs all examples
sequentially via `tsx`, with:
- **Skip** logic for infrastructure-dependent examples (TLS, cluster,
ClickHouse Cloud, production long-running demo)
- **Graceful SIGTERM** for examples designed to run indefinitely
(`async_insert_without_waiting.ts`,
`node/insert_streaming_with_backpressure.ts`) — runs them for 5 s then
stops them
  - Non-zero exit on any failure with full stdout/stderr output
- **`examples/package.json`** — added `run-examples` script
- **`.github/workflows/tests.yml`** — new `run-examples` job (matrix:
`head`/`latest` ClickHouse), added to the `success` gate

### Bug fixes in examples

1. **`examples/custom_json_handling.ts`** — `valueSerializer` only
handled top-level `BigInt`/`Date`/`Array`, so inserting an object like
`{ id: BigInt(...), dt: new Date() }` threw `TypeError: Do not know how
to serialize a BigInt`. Fixed by adding recursive object traversal:
   ```ts
   if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
     return Object.fromEntries(
       Object.entries(value).map(([k, v]) => [k, valueSerializer(v)]),
     )
   }
   ```

2. **`examples/node/insert_streaming_with_backpressure.ts`** — `CREATE
OR REPLACE ${tableName}` was missing the `TABLE` keyword, causing a
ClickHouse syntax error (`code 62`).

## Checklist

- [ ] Unit and integration tests covering the common scenarios were
added
- [x] A human-readable description of the changes was provided to
include in CHANGELOG
- [ ] For significant changes, documentation in
https://github.com/ClickHouse/clickhouse-docs was updated with further
explanations or tutorials

tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: peter-leonov-ch <209667683+peter-leonov-ch@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Warn on connection TTL being greater than the server keep-alive timeout (#597)

## Summary

Adding a descriptive warning log for cases when the client and server
disagree on keep-alive connection timeout. By design the client should
respect the timeout coming from the server and keep it smaller to avoid
race conditions.

Next steps:
* try setting the timeout from the server response when the TTL from the
config is not defined

## Checklist

Delete items not relevant to your PR:

- [x] Unit and integration tests covering the common scenarios were
added
- [ ] A human-readable description of the changes was provided to
include in CHANGELOG

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Vibe hero (#40)

* feat: add vibe background to hero section

- YouTube IFrame (IVugCjwIqKw) plays muted at vol 15 on desktop
- VibeContext/VibeProvider/useVibe for shared state
- VibeBackground: desktop-only, hides cleanly when toggled off
  (opacity + visibility transition avoids dark overlay leak)
- VibeControls: animated bars visualizer + volume slider + toggle
  placed in top-right chrome before lang switcher (desktop-only)
- vibe.css: CSS variable overrides scoped to [data-vibe-active=1]
  to keep hero text white over dark video in light theme
- Responsive: both components return null on mobile (< 768px)
- No performance impact on mobile; zero blocking Lighthouse score
- 5% iframe-wrapper size buffer prevents black edge artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore vibe hero readability in light mode

gray.700 was set to 20% opacity (nearly invisible), breaking the
CopyCommand text and GitHub button label when vibe is active in
light theme. Fix:
- gray.700 → rgb(255 255 255 / 85%) for readable white text
- gray.50  → rgb(0 0 0 / 45%) to flip CopyCommand bg to dark glass
- gray.200 → rgb(255 255 255 / 20%) for subtle white border

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(vibe): listening handshake, sync dispatch, gradient button, loop, arrow

- Add YouTube listening handshake in iframe onLoad so YouTube delivers
  onReady/onStateChange events and accepts postMessage commands (setVolume,
  unMute, seekTo, playVideo) — root cause of both the silent-volume and
  no-loop bugs

- Create vibe-player-registry lib singleton (registerVibePlayerCommand /
  sendVibePlayerCommand) so VibeProvider can dispatch player commands
  synchronously inside user-gesture handlers, preserving browser autoplay-
  with-sound policy without async useEffect lag

- Fix volumeRef update: move from render-time assignment to useEffect to
  satisfy react-hooks/refs; reorder hooks to resolve section-order lint

- Fix video loop: handle onStateChange info=0 with seekTo(0)+playVideo
  before YouTube shows end-screen suggestions; remove loop=1&playlist
  URL params that caused suggestion overlay between loops

- Replace volume/mute icon button with gradient 'Vibe on/off' text button
  (linear-gradient blue→indigo→purple matching video palette); 'on'/'off'
  words rendered in italic via <em>

- Remove Box wrapper around ScrollIndicator; add zIndex={1} to root Box
  in scroll-indicator so arrow renders above vibe overlay without overlap

- Update hero title: 'Vibe Coding / From Prompt to IPO' (EN+TH), remove
  trailing punctuation; update subtitle to match new title

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Refactor LandingHero component to accept props for title, subtitle, and getStarted text

feat: Update vibe-background to improve YouTube video looping and add helper functions

test: Add unit tests for sendYouTubeCommand helper function

fix: Remove unnecessary types file for vibe-background

chore: Introduce constants for initial vibe state and install command

fix: Update report-error action to correctly import schema from new path

* feat: add VibeControls component with desktop support and volume control

- Introduced VibeControls component with props for desktop detection, vibe status, and volume control.
- Updated VibeControls stories to include various states (default, vibe off, mobile).
- Enhanced VibeControls tests to verify rendering behavior based on desktop and vibe states.

feat: create ContainerCopyCommand for clipboard interaction

- Implemented ContainerCopyCommand to handle copying commands to clipboard.
- Added tests to verify button rendering and clipboard functionality.

feat: refactor landing hero to use ContainerPageChrome

- Replaced PageChrome with ContainerPageChrome in ContainerWelcomePage.
- Updated tests to mock ContainerPageChrome instead of PageChrome.

feat: implement ContainerVibeBackground for YouTube integration

- Created ContainerVibeBackground to manage YouTube iframe interactions.
- Added helper functions for sending commands to the YouTube player.
- Implemented tests to verify mobile rendering behavior.

feat: add ButtonGoBackClient for navigation

- Introduced ButtonGoBackClient to handle back navigation with a button.
- Updated ButtonGoBack to accept an onClick prop for flexibility.
- Refactored NotFound component to use ButtonGoBackClient.

commit.message:
feat: wire combo system — EventBus signal + fight_scene integration (#84)

Add combo_updated signal to EventBus so combo_tracker.gd emissions
are received by HUD and other systems. Wire combo_tracker into
fight_scene.gd as a child node with both fighters registered.

Combo tracker counts consecutive hits per fighter, resets on opponent
recovery (idle/walk/crouch/jump), timeout (60 frames), or blocked hit.
Emits combo_updated, hit_confirmed, and combo_ended through EventBus.

Closes #68

commit.message:
perf: RenderPlan struct-of-arrays with ordinal binding (#1306)

Replace array-of-structs (RenderSegment[]) with struct-of-arrays:
parallel byte[] ops, byte[][] fragments, string[] tokenKeys, int[]
ordinals, bool[] isRawHtml arrays indexed by segment ordinal.

Bind() resolves token names to ordinal indices on first Execute():
- op 0: static fragment (direct copy)
- op 1/4: page value (ordinal into values[])
- op 2/5: app value (ordinal into appvalues[])
- op 3: byte-rendered value (ordinal into byteValues[])
- op 6: unresolved (dropped)

After bind, Execute() is pure switch + array[ordinal] — zero string
comparisons on the hot render path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update websockets.connect(): ping_interval=None, ping_timeout=None,

  ping/pong cycle and handles large combined-stream payloads

test: momentum resource coverage (#1274)

Activates momentum test coverage after #1293 and #1295 merge.

**Changes:**
- Removes file-scoped MomentumResource stub (real class now in Dungnz.Models)
- Group 1 (8 unit tests): Add/Reset/IsCharged/Consume — all pass with real class
- Group 2 (4 player init tests): Rewritten to test CombatEngine.InitPlayerMomentum — Warrior max=5, Mage max=3, Rogue null, Ranger max=3 ✅
- Group 3 (6 integration tests): 2 active, 4 skipped with detailed reasons
  - Warrior increment: PlayerDied path preserves momentum for assertion ✅
  - Warrior WI-D: 'Momentum unleashed' message assertion across 7 rounds ✅
  - Mage (2): skipped — menu nav complexity + InitPlayerMomentum reset issue
  - Ranger (2): skipped — minimum-damage-1 rule prevents 0-damage scenario

**Key discovery:** ResetCombatEffects (called on Won) → ResetCombatPassives → Momentum?.Reset(). Post-Won momentum always 0. PlayerDied path or message-assertion are the viable test strategies.

Test count: 1858 → 1872 passing (+14), 4 skipped

Closes part of #1274

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: add compiled render plan + arena renderer + instrumentation (#1306)

Three rendering strategies now available:

1. Original: re-parses {{tokens}} per request via PipeWriter streaming
2. Arena: re-parses into ArrayBufferWriter (single contiguous buffer)
3. Compiled: pre-parsed RenderPlan[] of static UTF-8 fragments + token
   holes. Zero-copy path writes directly to response PipeWriter when
   no compression is needed; falls back to compiled+buffered for
   compressed responses.

RenderPage auto-selects the optimal path:
- No compression/banner/loops → compiled plan streams directly to
  PipeWriter (zero intermediate allocation)
- Compression needed → compiled plan renders to ArrayBufferWriter,
  then compress + write once

Instrumentation:
- RenderStats struct tracks per-render: write count, bytes written,
  flush count, token count, fragment count
- Thread-local counters in all Write methods
- OnRenderCompleteWithStats callback for detailed telemetry

Also adds HtmlRenderBenchmarks (BenchmarkDotNet) comparing all four
paths: Original_PipeWriter, Arena_SingleBuffer, Compiled_ZeroCopy,
Compiled_Buffered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add missing Swagger docs for chat, feedback, and conversation endpoints (#1072)

The Swagger UI was missing documentation for the core chat flow
endpoints — the SSE connection, message sending, stream stop, and status
endpoints — plus feedback and conversation management routes.

## Changes

### `server/routes/chat/sessionRoutes.js`
- Added reusable schema components: `ChatMessage`, `ChatRequest`,
`ChatStreamingResponse`, `ChatErrorResponse`
- Documented all four previously undocumented endpoints:
- `GET /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}` — SSE event-stream connection
(clients connect here first)
- `POST /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}` — send a chat message; streams via
SSE if connected, otherwise returns directly
- `POST /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}/stop` — abort the active LLM request
and close the stream
- `GET /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}/status` — connection/processing
status

### `server/routes/chat/feedbackRoutes.js`
- Documented `POST /feedback` with full request body schema (rating in
0.5 increments, optional comment, etc.)

### `server/routes/chat/conversationRoutes.js`
- Replaced plain JSDoc comments with `@swagger` annotations for all
three conversation endpoints:
  - `GET /apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`
- `DELETE
/apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`
  - `DELETE /apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}`

The Normal API spec now exposes 19 documented paths (up from 11).

commit.message:
Update vite.config.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 16: FieldId DU, generic Focus.tabOrder, benchmark CI, undo algebraic laws

- Add [<Struct>] FieldId DU and FieldId module (create/value) to Widgets.fs
- All Form and TextForm APIs now use FieldId instead of string for type safety
- Focus.tabOrder made generic ('a list -> 'a -> FocusDirection -> 'a)
- TFFieldErrors and TFSetFieldErrors use Map<FieldId,string>
- Obsolete attributes on Form.fieldFromKey/handleKey now include 'Will be removed in v1.0.'
- README test count corrected: 1,112 -> 1,526
- Benchmark regression CI job added to .github/workflows/ci.yml
  (benchmark-action/github-action-benchmark, 500% alert threshold, fail-on-alert)
- TextEditorTests: added custom FsCheck Arbitraries (SingleLineContent, ContentMsg)
- TextEditorTests: 4 algebraic law property tests for undo/redo subsystem
  Law1: undo(edit(x)).content = x.content (left inverse)
  Law2: undo at empty history is identity (idempotent)
  Law3: redo(undo(edit(x))).content = edit(x).content (right inverse)
  Law4: history depth never exceeds updateWithUndoDepth cap (bounded)
- All 1,530 tests passing (was 1,526; +4 new law tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: combo tracking system with EventBus signals

Add combo_tracker.gd that counts consecutive hits per fighter,
resets on opponent recovery/block/timeout, and emits combo_updated
and hit_confirmed signals through EventBus. Wired into fight_scene.gd
as a child node registered with both fighters.

Closes #68

feat: complete rebuild — Streamlit dashboard, Flask API, Chrome extension, GitHub Pages

- Rebuilt data_preprocessing.py for FakeNewsNet.csv (title/real columns)
- Rebuilt model_training.py with balanced Logistic Regression (F1: 67.1%)
- Professional Streamlit app with Plotly charts, URL/batch analysis, model insights
- Flask REST API with /api/predict and /api/predict-url endpoints
- Chrome Manifest V3 browser extension for one-click page analysis
- GitHub Pages showcase site with live model metrics
- GitHub Actions workflow for automatic Pages deployment
- Updated requirements.txt, README.md, test_app.py, run_app.py

feat(RS-201,RS-202): wave breakdown in CP overview, DNS excluded from counts

commit.message:
fix(desktop): use explicit period flag

Harden the desktop-to-Go period handoff by sending tray period selections through a named --period argument instead of relying on positional numeric args. Add Go and Rust regression coverage for named period handling, precedence, and desktop sidecar argument generation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.60: OSM voxel carving — tunnels, aeroways, building rotation, server DHT fixes

- terrain.rs: tunnel roads now carve underground AIR passages (5-voxel clearance)
  with ASPHALT floor instead of being skipped; surface terrain unchanged above
- terrain.rs: aeroway carving pass — runways (45m CONCRETE), taxiways (23m),
  aprons/helipads from OsmAeroway ways; surface flattened to grade
- terrain.rs: bridges still elevated in render pipeline (unchanged)
- world_inference.rs: building rotation computed from longest polygon edge angle
  (rotation_y = atan2 of longest edge direction) instead of always 0.0
- server: OSM DHT announce now reads from TileStore (iter_osm_coords) instead
  of scanning legacy flat .bin files
- server: cleanup_old_srtm_dir now called at startup alongside cleanup_old_tile_dir
  to remove old flat elevation files after TileStore migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
